demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis demographics against facility face sheet; corrected patient phone number (digit transposition) and verified Medicare Advantage PPO status. Home address in urban Pennsylvania confirmed via daughter/POA.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker confirmed the goal is Home with Home Health Agency (HHA) support for CHF management.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient's edema is resolving; SW indicates a target discharge for Friday. PT progress is steady but patient fatigue remains a factor.
v_card_and_flyer_status: Program flyer delivered to patient; V-Card successfully inserted into the daughter's (POA) phone during a bedside education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter 24 hours prior to discharge; home is ready with O2 in place, but expressed concern that the HHA has not confirmed a specific arrival time for the nurse.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date (Friday) and projected 1st visit date (Monday) into Atlantis; gap noted due to HHA intake volume.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit attempted for Saturday but blocked; HHA intake coordinator cited HHA_SOC_Overlap and staffing constraints, pushing the start-of-care to Monday.
narrative_summary: The patient, a 78-year-old female with CHF, was triaged in Atlantis with corrected demographics to ensure seamless contact. During maintenance, the PN provided education on transition logistics and secured V-card placement with the POA. However, a Friday discharge coincided with an HHA intake freeze, resulting in a scheduling overlap that pushed the first visit beyond the 24-hour incentive window.

format_1_state_log.event_description: HHA reports intake staffing freeze for weekend admissions.
PN verifies daughter's availability for a Monday morning visit instead of Saturday.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis demographics for patient address mismatched the facility face sheet.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Updated Atlantis record to match verified face sheet address before HHA referral.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA intake coordinator reports inability to staff a Saturday start-of-care.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated the coverage gap to the Social Worker to determine if Friday discharge was still clinically safe without weekend monitoring.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's daughter expresses anxiety about managing the CHF scale/weight log.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated daughter on the role of the Medical Assistant and the physical appointment card contact info.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon learning of the HHA's staffing overlap, the PN logs the delay in Atlantis and defers to the Social Worker to finalize the discharge date.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's authority over discharge timing and HHA selection while maintaining documentation in the system of record.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN flags the Friday discharge gap to the SW and simultaneously contacts the HHA to request a Monday morning 'first-slot' priority for the MA visit.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the Stage 3 checklist by identifying scheduling conflicts and coordinating with the home team to minimize the transition gap.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN calls a secondary HHA agency to see if they have weekend availability and suggests the SW switch providers to ensure the 24-hour incentive is met.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Bypasses the SW's role in agency selection and prioritizes program incentives over the established facility referral process.
